subject: Agricultural Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 6.0 LIVESTOCK PRODUCTION
topics: anatomy and physiology for poultry livestock and rearing of broilers from day-old to maturity.
activities: observing appropriate management practices such as feeding, housing, health care, and hygiene. In addition, learners will examine
methods of adding value to poultry products and explore effective marketing strategies. They will also analyse yields and assess the
quality of poultry products with the aim of identifying areas of improvement. Furthermore, learners will be encouraged to investigate
innovative approaches that can enhance productivity and efficiency in poultry production.
Demonstrate understanding of anatomy and physiology for poultry livestock
resources: Arrange a visit to a local poultry farm.
AGRICULTURAL SCIENCE TEACHING MODULE FORM 2 TERM 1 &
Learning Materials Needed: Internet access or printed information, Map of Zambia, Flip charts or notebooks, Markers/pens
Activity 1: Exploring poultry hatcheries in Zambia
assessment: In Zambia, poultry farming is a key component of livestock production that contributes to household nutrition, income generation, and
employment. Communities are increasingly adopting poultry rearing as a business, and learners are expected to acquire practical and
theoretical knowledge of poultry production. As a learner with knowledge of poultry production, management, and farm construction,
you are invited to prepare a presentation and practical demonstration for a school-based poultry awareness program.
i. Describe poultry and explain the importance of poultry production in Zambia;
ii. Categorise chickens according to their breeds and usage (e.g., meat, eggs, dual purpose);
